description: Conservative preservation pack for RemObjects Data Abstract and Remoting SDK components. Most components are nonvisual service/data-access components and may be preserved when the target project references compatible RemObjects packages.

rules.notes: Remote data adapter is nonvisual and may be preserved with compatible Data Abstract packages.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify schema, service names, login flow, dataset usage, and target-platform support.

rules.notes: Memory table is nonvisual and may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review fields, indexes, update logic, and binding strategy.

rules.notes: Data table component may be preserved with Data Abstract packages.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review data adapters, field definitions, and update behavior.

rules.notes: Schema component is nonvisual and may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify schema resource deployment and compatibility.

rules.notes: Message component is nonvisual and usually portable within RemObjects-supported targets.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify package references and channel compatibility.

rules.notes: SOAP message component is nonvisual and may be preserved.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify service endpoints, serialization, and deployment requirements.

rules.notes: HTTP channel may be preserved when Indy/RemObjects packages support the target.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review TLS, certificates, proxy behavior, and platform networking permissions.
WinInet is Windows-specific and may not be appropriate for cross-platform FMX targets.

rules.notes: TCP channel may be preserved with compatible packages.
rules.manual_review_reason: Review network permissions, TLS, timeouts, and platform behavior.

rules.notes: Local channel may be preserved in compatible application architectures.
rules.manual_review_reason: Verify server/client lifetime and threading model.
Event receiver threading and UI synchronization must be reviewed for FMX.
Visual branding/button component should be replaced or rebuilt in FMX.
